paper.title: Simulation-based Optimization for Augmented Reading
paper.abstract: Augmented reading systems aim to adapt text presentation to improve comprehension and task performance, yet existing approaches rely heavily on heuristics, opaque data-driven models, or repeated human involvement in the design loop. We propose framing augmented reading as a simulation-based optimization problem grounded in resource-rational models of human reading. These models instantiate a simulated reader that allocates limited cognitive resources, such as attention, memory, and time under ta

memory_insight.before_after_comparison: Reduced memory usage by 30% after applying compression and quantization

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing a hybrid offline-online optimization pipeline can reduce human testing time by 50% and improve design quality by 25%.
technical_details: Online approach using ongoing interaction data
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices, but may require additional optimization for mobile devices with limited RAM.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
